inputs.reasoning: Nested repetitions create complex NFA states that exercise the BitState stack and kInstAlt opcode handling in the main TrySearch loop.
Large counted repetitions generate many instructions, forcing stack growth and triggering the GrowStack() path when njob_ >= maxjob_.
Character class with ByteRange instructions tests the kInstByteRange case and the condition (p < end) to read characters.
Named captures exercise kInstCapture opcode with arg==0 case, testing capture register logic and the Push mechanism.
Multiple alternations with kInstAlt require the arg==0 and arg==1 switch cases to properly handle branching and re-pushing.
Sequence of optional elements creates a state machine that exercises the main loop condition (njob_ > 0) and CheckAndLoop logic.
Counted range repetitions generate kInstAltMatch instructions, testing the greedy() branch and non-greedy path selection.
Negated character class tests ByteRange matching logic with inverted sense, exercising the Matches() check with various character values.
Nested alternation and repetition forces stack growth and exercises the kInstAlt arg switch with fallthrough to error case.
Character class shortcuts with the if(0) CheckAndLoop label test the optimization path and ShouldVisit checking in the main loop.
